card_name: Broken Limits Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku
ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly reads 'Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku'. DBSCG official database confirms SD2-02 as a battle card from THE EXTREME EVOLUTION starter deck featuring SSJ3 Goku at 25000 power. The SSJ3 transformation is Goku-exclusive within Dragon Ball Z/Super (no other character in this TCG set holds SSJ3). Visual markers — no eyebrows, floor-length golden hair — are the canonical diagnostic of SSJ3 in Toriyama's original design (Daizenshu). Identity unambiguous.

trivia_bullets.text: SD2-02 is a battle card exclusive to the 'THE EXTREME EVOLUTION' Starter Deck (DBS-SD02) from the Cross Worlds era. At a printed power of 25000 and energy cost of 6, it represents SSJ3 as an expensive, high-power finisher in the starter's Red color identity. Starter-exclusive cards like this are not available in booster packs. [WebSearch snippet: dbs-cardgame.com card database]

The Super Saiyan 3 transformation debuted in Dragon Ball Z during the Majin Buu Saga. Toriyama's concept art (documented in the Daizenshu encyclopedia) always featured the pronounced brow ridge and absence of eyebrows — a deliberate design choice to distinguish SSJ3 from SSJ and SSJ2, making the face more feral and the form's silhouette immediately readable. [CBR: 'Why DBZ's Super Saiyan 3 Is Missing Eyebrows'; Kanzenshuu forum via WebSearch]
sources: kanzenshuu.com/forum SSJ3 design thread via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Within Dragon Ball Z lore, SSJ3 is notorious as Goku's most powerful base-anime transformation that nonetheless never achieved a decisive victory — it stalled Majin Buu but did not defeat him, and the form's massive ki drain made it impractical. The card's name 'Broken Limits' foregrounds the transformation's narrative function as a threshold-breaker rather than a reliable weapon. [Game Rant: 'Dragon Ball Super Saiyan 3, Explained' via WebSearch; Screen Rant SSJ3 analysis via WebSearch]

SSJ3's no-eyebrows visual is a known identification challenge in TCG card art — cards depicting different SSJ3 characters (Goku, Gotenks, Gohan in some media) share the same silhouette. The card title 'Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku' resolves the ambiguity explicitly, which is standard practice in the DBSCG card naming convention. [CBR: 'Every Super Saiyan 3 User' via WebSearch]
